commit.message:
[Repo Assist] Add missing C# extension methods for Frame.Stack, Unstack, MeltBy, JoinOn, Compare, and more (#702)

🤖 *This is an automated pull request from Repo Assist, an AI assistant
for this repository.*

---

## Summary

Several `Frame` operations added to the F# module in recent releases
(4.0.x / 5.0.x) were not exposed as C# extension methods in
`FrameExtensions.fs`. This PR fills those gaps so C# consumers can call
them via the same fluent `frame.Method(...)` style as all other
extension methods.

### New extension methods

| Method | F# equivalent | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `frame.Stack()` | `Frame.stack` | Wide → long format (row×col 2-tuple
index) |
| `frame.Unstack()` | `Frame.unstack` | Long → wide format (inverse of
Stack) |
| `frame.MeltBy(idCols...)` | `Frame.meltBy` | pandas-style melt with
identity columns |
| `frame.WhereRowsByMask(mask)` | `Frame.filterRowsByMask` |
Boolean-mask row filtering |
| `frame.WhereColsByMask(mask)` | `Frame.filterColsByMask` |
Boolean-mask column filtering |
| `frame.RenameColumn(old, new)` | `Frame.renameCol` | Rename a single
column |
| `frame.RenameColumns(mapping)` | `Frame.renameColsUsing` | Rename all
columns via a `Func` |
| `frame.JoinOn(other, kind, col)` | `Frame.joinOn` | Join on a column
value (generic row key) |
| `frame.JoinOnString(other, kind, col)` | `Frame.joinOnString` | Join
on column value → `string` row key |
| `frame.JoinOnInt(other, kind, col)` | `Frame.joinOnInt` | Join on
column value → `int` row key |
| `frame.Compare(other)` | `Frame.compare` | Column-by-column `Diff`
frame |

---

## Design notes

- All methods are thin wrappers calling the existing F# module functions
— no new logic.
- `MeltBy` takes a `[<ParamArray>]` so C# callers can write
`frame.MeltBy("id1", "id2")`.
- `JoinOn` / `JoinOnString` / `JoinOnInt` accept `JoinKind` as the
second argument, consistent with the existing `Join` overloads.
- `RenameColumns` accepts a `Func<TColumnKey, TColumnKey2>` to allow
changing the column key type (consistent with the F# `renameColsUsing`).

---

## Test Status

- `dotnet test tests/Deedle.Tests/Deedle.Tests.fsproj -c Release` →
**703 tests passed, 0 failed**
- `dotnet test tests/Deedle.CSharp.Tests/Deedle.CSharp.Tests.csproj -c
Release` → **29 tests passed, 0 failed**

commit.message:
ux: turn indicator, score tracking, animations, cell shake

- Turn indicator above board: shows current player symbol + name,
  colored red/blue to match X/O; hidden on game over
- Session score label below board: tracks X wins, draws, O wins
  across games; persists until scene is torn down
- Move pop-in: symbol scales 0→1.2→1.0 on placement for tactile
  feel
- Winning line fade-in: line animates from alpha 0→1 over 0.25s
- Game over overlay: container node fades + slides up from board;
  uses container pattern for clean single-node removal on reset
- Cell shake on taken: brief 3-frame lateral shake when tapping an
  occupied cell, replacing silent no-op
- Animated reset: symbols scale to 0 before removal; game over
  container fades out; board rebuilds after 0.18s delay so
  animations complete before state is cleared
- Board intro: boardNode fades in on initial load

commit.message:
Implement spec-compliant ResolveExport for module resolution validation

Add validate_module_resolution() with the full ResolveExport algorithm
(ES2024 §16.2.1.16.3) to detect module link-time errors:
- Circular re-exports (resolve set cycle detection)
- Missing named re-export bindings (returns null)
- Ambiguous star-export bindings (multiple different resolutions)

Key spec nuances handled:
- import-then-export reclassification: 'import {x} from m; export {x}'
  is traced through to the import source as an indirect export entry
- Namespace re-exports: 'export * as ns from m' and
  'import * as ns from m; export {ns}' both resolve to the target
  module's namespace binding, enabling correct ambiguity comparison
- Star exports skip 'default' per spec step 9

Uses ModuleResolutionContext struct to thread per-module maps:
local_exports, import_bindings, export_to_local, all_reexport_deps.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Phase 2: Handshake Protocol Types and Transport Trait Extension (#142)

* feat: add handshake protocol types and Transport trait extension

Phase 2 of the version-compatibility implementation plan.

Adds handshake types for version negotiation between cluster nodes:

system_actors.rs:
- HandshakeRequest: node_id, wire_version (WireVersion), app_version, adapter
- HandshakeResponse: Accepted { ... } | Rejected { reason, detail }
- RejectionReason: IncompatibleProtocol, IncompatibleAdapter
- validate_handshake(): compares local vs remote config, returns response
- 8 unit tests (accept, reject protocol/adapter, priority order, field check)

proto/system.proto:
- HandshakeRequestProto, HandshakeResponseProto (oneof accepted/rejected)
- HandshakeAcceptedProto, HandshakeRejectedProto
- RejectionReasonProto enum
- All field numbers frozen per wire protocol convention

proto.rs:
- encode/decode_handshake_request: roundtrip with WireVersion parsing
- encode/decode_handshake_response: oneof Accepted/Rejected with validation
- rejection_reason_to_proto/from_proto conversions
- 8 proto roundtrip + validation tests

transport.rs:
- Transport::handshake() default returns error (safe for adapters not yet updated)
- InMemoryTransport: handshake_info map, set_handshake_info(), link() shares info
- InMemoryTransport::handshake() validates via validate_handshake()
- 5 transport handshake tests (accept, reject, no info, default trait)

lib.rs:
- Re-exports HandshakeRequest, HandshakeResponse, RejectionReason, validate_handshake

Total: 21 new tests, zero clippy warnings, all 506 existing tests pass.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: address multi-model review findings for Phase 2

- Fix validate_handshake arg order in InMemoryTransport::handshake()
  (ChatGPT+Haiku+Gemini: response contained caller's info, not remote's)
- Add empty-field validation for wire_version/adapter in decode_handshake_response
  (Sonnet+Haiku: inconsistent with decode_handshake_request validation)
- Fix doc on set_handshake_info: info is copied at link time, not shared
  (ChatGPT+Gemini: post-link updates not visible to already-linked peers)
- Add 4 new proto validation tests (empty adapter, empty wire_version)
- Strengthen transport test: assert response node_id is remote, not caller

Private fields finding (ChatGPT) declined: Rust enum variant fields
are always public. No code change needed.

commit.message:
SEO: Remove 44 shadowed routes, clean config for Azure SWA

Previous deploy failed: Azure SWA rejects configs where specific routes
are shadowed by wildcard routes (e.g. /full-courses/az900fullcourse
shadowed by /full-courses/*).

Fix: Remove all 44 specific routes now covered by wildcards.
Result: 72 clean routes, 0 duplicates, 0 shadowing.

Wildcard redirect mapping:
- /hiking/* → / (old personal content)
- /books/* → / (old personal content)
- /interview-questions/* → /interview-prep/
- /study-guides/* → /cert-tracker/
- /bootcamps/* → /cloud-labs/
- /guest/* → /cloud-labs/
- /full-courses/* → /certifications/
- /ai-hub/v/* → /ai-hub/

commit.message: 🗃️ fix: ensure all preferences and filters are applied end-to-end 🗃️ - enabledEngines preference: apply saved engine list as &engines= param in streamResults() and infinite scroll URLs so the user's engine selection actually restricts which engines are queried - minDuration preference: send &min_duration= to server; server now reads the param in APISearch and passes it through handleSearchSSE to SearchStreamWithOperators, which applies max(config, user) threshold - maxHistory preference: replace hardcoded history cap of 20 with the user's maxHistory pref (0 = no limit, hard-capped at 200) - autoClearHistory preference: implemented in getHomeSearchHistory(); entries older than the configured number of days are removed and the trimmed list is persisted back to localStorage - proxyImages preference: thumbnail src now routes through /proxy/thumbnails?url= when proxyImages is true (the default) for privacy; direct URL used only when user explicitly opts out - preview_first: also sent on infinite scroll page loads (was missing) - Engine tier filter (home page): handleSearchSubmit injects a hidden input so the filter-engines dropdown value ("tier1", "tier12") is included in the form GET submission - getEnginesToUse: support "tier1" and "tier12" as special values that filter to engines whose Tier() is ≤ 1 or ≤ 2 respectively Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com> src/server/handler/handlers.go src/server/service/engine/manager.go src/server/static/js/app.js

commit.message:
Expand UI-D-33 Notification/Responsive/A11y spec from stub to full spec

Expand the cross-cutting specification from ~246-line stub (v1.0) to full
~1450-line spec (v2.0) covering all 13 required template sections:

- Notification Center: bell icon dropdown, full /notifications page,
  6 notification categories with color palette, WebSocket real-time push,
  unread badge (99+ cap), quiet hours, preference management
- Responsive Design: 5 breakpoints (sm→2xl), sidebar collapse behavior,
  12-column grid system, per-component responsive rules
- Accessibility: WCAG 2.1 AA, ARIA landmarks, keyboard shortcuts,
  screen reader live regions, prefers-reduced-motion, colour-blind safeguards

Includes full TypeScript for Zustand notification store, TanStack Query
hooks, and WebSocket connection manager with reconnection strategy.
